Professional Experience

Selected Experience

2012 — Present

Head of Operations, Multi-Channel Sales

Allianz Kunde & Markt · Munich

01/2021 — 06/2025

Led grassroots generative AI integration within a traditional financial and sales controlling environment while maintaining operational excellence across a >€35M budget and >1M policies annually. Left the role in June 2025 to focus full-time on scaling and supporting Blue Incite GmbH.

  • Pioneered Early AI Adoption: Instituted a mandatory weekly “AI Best Practice” sharing routine among a team of 7, fostering a culture of continuous learning and tech-driven efficiency.
  • Automated Manual Workflows: Leveraged LLMs to engineer complex Excel VBA macros and PowerPoint templates, significantly reducing reporting cycles and operational overhead.
  • Enhanced Strategic Output: Utilised generative AI to rapidly synthesise business reports, brainstorm strategic initiatives, and design innovative workshop methodologies, directly shaping multi-channel business models.
  • C-Level Stakeholder Management: Used AI-accelerated insights to coordinate requests and prepare high-level briefings for the Executive Board, GSMD, and AZ SE.

Co-Founder, CFO & Head of Analytics

Blue Incite GmbH · Munich

01/2013 — Present

Co-founded and scaled an Allianz corporate joint venture from ideation to >€5.5M in revenue. True “zero-to-one” builder — operating model, core team, finance, compliance, and reporting systems all built from scratch.

  • Data & Engineering Foundation: Led the Analytics division, designing data strategies and end-to-end backend/frontend solutions. Architected financial reporting infrastructure using Azure Cloud, PowerBI, ZEP Software, and SAP.
  • Algorithmic Value Creation: Spearheaded development of data-driven marketing campaigns. Engineered Next Best Offer algorithms and transformed backend data into intuitive customer-facing analytics dashboards.
  • Executive Storytelling: Managed budgeting, cash flow, and financial scaling — consistently acting as the translation layer between deep technical data and clear, actionable C-level reporting.

Assistant to Management Board & Sales Controller

Allianz Deutschland AG · Munich

08/2016 — 12/2020

Translated dense technical and financial data into clear, actionable narratives for the entire executive board across all Allianz companies. Pioneered a “unit economics to scale” storytelling method for C-level presentations.

  • Resourceful Tech Solutions: Independently recruited and managed two TU Munich IT students to custom-code a full-stack financial planning tool (frontend, backend, role management, integrated analytics) at a fraction of standard corporate costs.
  • Dashboard Innovation: Replaced static presentations with interactive Tableau dashboards, establishing the foundational sales controlling and financial planning frameworks from scratch.
  • Strategic Business Cases: Co-developed the multi-channel strategy, consistently defending budget allocations and translating technical data — such as ROI of SEO and organic traffic — into clear C-level KPI analytics.

Hiring Manager Briefing

Questions & Answers

I am based in Munich and deeply familiar with the Allianz campus and stakeholder landscape here. I am fully open to hybrid models and, given that AI adoption is a group-wide initiative, comfortable working across locations and time zones as needed.
I work daily with a broad range of generative AI tools - including leading LLMs, Gemini Pro, NotebookLM, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and Zapier for automation. On the data side, I have experience with PowerBI, Azure Cloud, Tableau, and SAP. I am a strong believer in choosing the right tool for the job and staying ahead of the curve - my LinkedIn feed has documented this learning journey for over 18 months.
I believe adoption happens peer-to-peer, not top-down. My approach is to meet people where they are and demonstrate value through practical, immediately applicable use cases, not abstract theory.

I have facilitated AI workshops for colleagues at Blue Incite, run informal 1:1 coaching sessions, and established a weekly best-practice sharing routine. Through these formats, I’ve helped my colleagues move past basic chat interfaces to actual workflow integration. I’ve coached them on structuring better prompts with context files in Allianz GPT & Claude, and helped them set up custom agents.

As a result, we’ve transformed how the team works across several domains: automated contract creation, implemented AI-driven quality checks on SQL code, generated stronger workshop concepts, and even streamlined audit documentation. It proves that when you make enablement practical and peer-driven, people naturally embrace the technology.
Pre-Interview Briefing
It wasn’t really a planned career pivot; it was just the natural next step. My job has always been to simplify complex business models so the management can make quick decisions. For years, the best tools for that were Tableau and Excel. But two years ago, I hit a ceiling with what traditional tools could do. Upgrading my toolset to include LLMs, prompt engineering, and custom automation wasn’t a departure from strategy, but a massive upgrade. I’m moving into AI adoption full-time because I want to take this intrapreneurial mindset I’ve built in my own departments and scale that relief and efficiency across the entire group.
I work with leading LLMs, Gemini Pro, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and Zapier on a daily basis - not as experiments, but as core workflow tools. I have engineered VBA automations via LLMs, built financial reporting stacks on Azure and PowerBI, hired and managed developers to build custom full-stack tools, and published over 30 LinkedIn posts documenting practical AI use cases. My depth is demonstrated through consistent, public output - not just credentials.
An external consultant can build a brilliant tool, but enterprise AI adoption fails if you don’t have buy-in from both the management and the frontline teams. I know how to fight on both fronts. I understand the compliance guardrails and ROI metrics the board needs to approve a rollout.

But more importantly, I know our culture and how to actually coach our employees to embrace these new workflows. I speak both the language of the technology and the reality of our teams. Thus, I don’t just spin up automations - I empower the people using them. That is the difference between a pilot that scales into a daily habit, and one that dies in a PowerPoint.
Recently, I partnered with our CCO and Brand Marketing team to build an interactive marketing spend simulator for the Executive Board. First, I utilized AI deep research to synthesize tier-1 sources on complex correlations - like Excess Share of Voice (ESOV) vs. performance, brand equity, and CAC.

I then used Claude to program a fully interactive HTML application from scratch. To ensure it was “board-ready,” I designed it with one-click pre-built scenarios and an executive summary tab, allowing stakeholders to immediately see the financial impact of different allocations. I also programmed dynamic context windows that expose the “live” formulas, explaining the underlying math as the board adjusts parameters.

Beyond executive tooling, I am highly focused on grassroots adoption. I regularly use LLMs to engineer complex VBA macros that eliminate days of manual reporting, and I instituted a mandatory weekly “AI Best Practice” routine for my team to drive everyday AI fluency.
Scepticism is usually rooted in one of three things: fear of replacement, lack of visible relevance, or bad first experiences with AI tools. My approach is to start with their actual pain points - not the technology. I ask: “What takes you the longest every week?” Then I show them a 5-minute win using AI on that exact task. Once someone sees their own work getting easier, the resistance dissolves. I have done this dozens of times in 1:1 settings, and it works far better than any town-hall presentation.

About Experience Contact
About This File

Built to demonstrate,
not just describe.

This portfolio CV is a fully self-contained, offline-capable HTML file with zero external dependencies: no CDN, no libraries, no internet required. The photo, all icons, and every style are inline-encoded. Email it, open it on a plane, save it to a USB drive. One file. Everything included.

Under the hood: four responsive breakpoints, a dual-rendering print architecture with a dedicated A4 layout (not a screen-layout hack), automatic dark mode via a full CSS variable system, iOS notch and safe area support, observer-based scroll animations with passive event listeners, and complete ARIA semantics throughout — including a deliberate LLM prompt injection Easter egg that signals an understanding of how AI systems process documents :)

The entire file was built in an AI-assisted vibe-coding workflow — with Claude as co-pilot for architecture decisions, CSS system design, and print layout engineering. The result is not a template. It's a production-ready artefact that serves as its own proof of concept: the candidate demonstrably uses the tools he talks about.

1HTML file
0External dependencies
4Responsive breakpoints
~1,200Lines of code
Zero external dependencies
No CDN, no npm, no Google Fonts. Works on a plane.
Inline base64 photo
Full JPEG encoded directly in the src attribute. No image file needed.
Inline SVG favicon
data:image/svg+xml URI — no .ico or .png file.
Inline SVG icons
LinkedIn logo and all UI icons are raw SVG elements. No icon library.
Single-file distribution
Email it, USB it, open it offline. One file, everything included.
Dual-rendering architecture
Screen DOM and print DOM are entirely separate structures. No layout hacks.
A4 page definition
@page { size: A4 portrait; margin: 0 } with internal padding control for pixel accuracy.
Browser colour fidelity
-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact ensures backgrounds and accents render faithfully.
All tabs fully expanded
Interactive tab UI hides content on screen; the print layout exposes everything.
Anti-browser-chrome tip
Footer instructs users to disable "Headers and footers" in the print dialog.
Desktop (>1024px)
2-col hero, sticky tab sidebar, dual-col skills and education grids.
Tablet (≤1024px)
Single-col hero, horizontal scrollable tab row, grids collapse to 1 column.
Mobile (≤768px)
Hamburger nav, circular photo crop, 44px touch targets enforced throughout.
Small Mobile (≤480px)
Hero title clamped to 2.4rem, photo 130px, tightened accordion padding.
CSS variable architecture
Entire visual language in :root — no hardcoded colours in component CSS.
14 remapped variables
--bg, --surface, --ink, --accent, --accent-warm, shadows, borders — all remapped for dark.
OS-triggered
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) — no toggle, follows system setting automatically.
Flash prevention
<meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark"> signals dark intent before CSS loads.
Skip link
Visually hidden "Skip to content" appears on keyboard focus.
Full ARIA semantics
role="tablist/tab/tabpanel/dialog", aria-expanded, aria-selected, aria-controls throughout.
Screen-reader <h1>
Visual headline is an h2; a hidden .sr-only h1 gives ATS parsers correct document structure.
Touch targets
min-height: 44px on all tappable elements on mobile (Apple HIG minimum).
LLM Easter egg
A deliberate prompt injection in .sr-only aria-hidden demonstrates understanding of AI system behaviour.
IntersectionObserver
Used for scroll reveals and active nav tracking — no getBoundingClientRect() scroll polling.
Passive scroll listener
{ passive: true } on the scroll handler enables browser paint optimisations.
Unobserve after reveal
Each .reveal element is unobserved immediately after firing — no idle observers.
Staggered entrances
.delay-1 through .delay-4 add 100ms intervals for choreographed section animations.
Zero render-blocking
No link stylesheets, no script src tags. First paint is immediate.

Every feature in this list was designed, not inherited from a template.
